book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
John is expelled from his Church by a Synod held at Chalcedon on account of his Dispraise of Women.

ccel.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

source.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

title: John is expelled from his Church by a Synod held at Chalcedon on account of his Dispraise of Women.

source.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: When Epiphanius was gone, John was informed by some person that the Empress Eudoxia had stimulated Epiphanius against him.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: And being of a fiery temperament, and of a ready utterance, he soon after pronounced a public invective against women in general.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: The people readily took this as uttered indirectly against the empress and so the speech was laid hold of by evil-disposed persons, and reported to those in authority.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: At length on being informed of it the empress immediately complained to her husband, telling him that the insult offered to herself was equally an insult against him.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: The emperor therefore authorized Theophilus to convoke a Synod without delay against John; Severian also co-operated in promoting this, for he still retained his grudge against Chrysostom.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Not long time accordingly intervened before Theophilus arrived, having induced several bishops from different cities to accompany him; these however had been summoned by the emperor’s orders also.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Many of the bishops in Asia John had deposed when he went to Ephesus and ordained Heraclides.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Accordingly they all by previous agreement assembled at Chalcedon in Bithynia.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Cyrinus was at that time bishop of Chalcedon, an Egyptian by birth, who said many things to the bishops in disparagement of John, denouncing him as ‘the impious,’ ‘the haughty,’ ‘the inexorable.’

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: They indeed were very much satisfied at these denunciations.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: But Maruthas bishop of Mesopotamia having involuntarily trod on Cyrinus’ foot, he was severely hurt by it and was unable to embark with the rest for Constantinople, but remained behind at Chalcedon.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: The rest crossed over.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Now Theophilus had so openly avowed his hostility to John, that none of the clergy would go forth to meet him, or pay him the least honor; but some Alexandrian sailors happening to be on the spot—for at that time the grain transporting vessels were there—greeted him with joyful acclamations.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: He excused himself from entering the church, and took up his abode at one of the imperial mansions called ‘The Placidian.’

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Then on this account a torrent of accusations began to be poured forth against John; for no mention was now made of Origen, but all were intent on urging a variety of criminations, many of which were ridiculous.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Preliminary matters being thus settled, the bishops were convened in one of the suburbs of Chalcedon, a place called ‘The Oak,’ and immediately cited John to answer the charges which were brought against him.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: He also summoned Serapion the deacon; Tigris the eunuch presbyter, and Paul the reader, were likewise summoned to appear there with him, for these men were included in the impeachments, as participators in his guilt.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: And since John taking exception to those who had cited him, on the ground of their being his enemies, refused to attend, and demanded a general council, without delay they repeated their citation four times in succession; and as he persisted in his refusal to meet them as his judges, always giving the same answer, they condemned him, and deposed him without assigning any other cause for his deposition but that he refused to obey the summons.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: This decision on being announced towards evening, incited the people to a most alarming sedition; insomuch that they kept watch all night, and would by no means suffer him to be removed from the church, but cried out that his cause ought to be determined in a larger assembly.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: A decree of the emperor, however, commanded that he should be immediately expelled, and sent into exile; which as soon as John was apprised of, he voluntarily surrendered himself about noon unknown to the populace, on the third day after his condemnation: for he dreaded any insurrectionary movement on his account, and was accordingly led away.
